content.thinking: Let me start by exploring the unsloth codebase to understand how it's structured and how existing save/export methods work.

input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase. I need to understand:
1. The overall directory structure
2. Where save/export methods live (look for files related to saving, exporting, GGUF, adapters)
3. How GGUF export is implemented
4. How merged model saving works
5. How adapter saving works
6. How methods are registered/exposed to users

Start by listing the top-level directory, then dive into relevant files. Be thorough - read the actual implementation code for save methods. Look for files like save.py, export.py, or similar. Check __init__.py files to understand what's exposed publicly.

Please report:
- All relevant file paths
- Key function/method signatures
- How the save methods are structured (parameters, docstrings, return types)
- How they're registered (patched onto classes, imported, etc.)
- Any patterns used for model weight extraction

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

814	def try_execute(commands, force_complete = False):
815	    for command in commands:
816	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell = True, stdout = subprocess.PIPE, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT, bufsize = 1) as sp:
817	            for line in sp.stdout:
818	                line = line.decode("utf-8", errors = "replace")
819	                if "undefined reference" in line:
820	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
821	                elif "deprecated" in line:
822	                    return "CMAKE"
823	                elif "Unknown argument" in line:
824	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
825	                elif "***" in line:
826	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
827	                print(line, flush = True, end = "")
828	            pass
829	            if force_complete and sp.returncode is not None and sp.returncode != 0:
830	                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, sp.args)
831	        pass
832	    pass
833	    return None

837	def install_llama_cpp_old(version = -10):
838	    # Download the 10th latest release since the latest might be broken!
839	    # FALLBACK mechanism
840	    releases = subprocess.check_output(["git", "ls-remote", "--tags", "https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp.git"])
841	    releases = releases.decode("utf-8").replace("\t", " ").split("\n")
842	    for i, x in enumerate(releases):
843	        if "refs/tags/b" not in x: break
844	    releases = releases[:i]
845	    latest = releases[-1]
846	    version = releases[version].split(" ")[0]
847	
848	    # Check if the llama.cpp exists
849	    if os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
850	        print(
851	            "**[WARNING]** You have a llama.cpp directory which is broken.\n"\
852	            "Unsloth will DELETE the broken directory and install a new one.\n"\
853	            "Press CTRL + C / cancel this if this is wrong. We shall wait 30 seconds.\n"
854	        )
855	        import time
856	        for i in range(30):
857	            print(f"**[WARNING]** Deleting llama.cpp directory... {30-i} seconds left.")
858	            time.sleep(1)
859	        import shutil
860	        shutil.rmtree("llama.cpp", ignore_errors = True)
861	    pass
862	
863	    # Clone a specific commit
864	    # Also don't use the GPU!
865	    commands = [
866	        "git clone --recursive https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp",
867	        f"cd llama.cpp && git reset --hard {version} && git clean -df",
868	    ]
869	    try_execute(commands)
870	
871	    # Try using MAKE
872	    commands = [
873	        "make clean -C llama.cpp",
874	        f"make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
875	    ]
876	    if try_execute(commands) == "CMAKE":
877	        # Instead use CMAKE
878	        commands = [
879	            "cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON",
880	            f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
881	            "cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp",
882	            "rm -rf llama.cpp/build",
883	        ]
884	        try_execute(commands)
885	    pass
886	
887	    # Check if successful
888	    if not (
889	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe") or
890	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize") or
891	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe") or
892	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize") or
893	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize") or
894	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize")
895	    ):
896	        raise RuntimeError(
897	            "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
898	            "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
899	            "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

1618	def create_ollama_modelfile(tokenizer, gguf_location):
1619	    """
1620	        Creates an Ollama Modelfile.
1621	        Use ollama.create(model = "new_ollama_model", modelfile = modelfile)
1622	    """
1623	    modelfile = getattr(tokenizer, "_ollama_modelfile", None)
1624	    if modelfile is None: return None
1625	
1626	    FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER = "⚫@✅#🦥__FILE_LOCATION__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1627	    EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER     = "⚫@✅#🦥__EOS_TOKEN__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1628	    LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER  = "⚫@✅#🦥"
1629	    RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER = "⚡@🦥#⛵"
1630	
1631	    # Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1087
1632	    # We must convert all {'s and }'s but keep {__FILE_LOCATION__} intact
1633	    modelfile = modelfile\
1634	        .replace("{__FILE_LOCATION__}", FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER)\
1635	        .replace("{__EOS_TOKEN__}",     EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER)\
1636	        .replace("{", LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER)\
1637	        .replace("}", RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER)
1638	
1639	    # Revert {__FILE_LOCATION__} back
1640	    modelfile = modelfile\
1641	        .replace(FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER, "{__FILE_LOCATION__}")\
1642	        .replace(EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER,     "{__EOS_TOKEN__}")
1643	
1644	    if "__EOS_TOKEN__" in modelfile:
1645	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1646	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1647	            __EOS_TOKEN__      = tokenizer.eos_token,
1648	        )
1649	    else:
1650	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1651	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1652	        )
1653	    pass
1654	
1655	    modelfile = modelfile\
1656	        .replace("⚫@✅#🦥", "{")\
1657	        .replace("⚡@🦥#⛵", "}")\
1658	        .rstrip()
1659	
1660	    return modelfile
1661	pass

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report with all my findings.

## Unsloth Codebase - Complete Save/Export Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the Unsloth codebase and mapped out the entire save/export system. Here's my comprehensive report:

### 1. **Directory Structure**

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main entry point - imports save, models, trainer
├── save.py                        # CORE FILE: 2678 lines - all save/export logic
├── trainer.py                     # Training integration, TRL patching
├── chat_templates.py
├── tokenizer_utils.py
├── models/
│   ├── __init__.py               # Imports FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, etc.
│   ├── llama.py
│   ├── mistral.py
│   ├── loader.py                 # FastLanguageModel and FastVisionModel
│   ├── rl.py
│   ├── _utils.py
│   └── [other model architectures]
├── kernels/                       # Contains fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE
├── registry/
├── utils/
└── dataprep/
```

### 2. **Save Methods Overview**

The system exposes **6 primary save methods** that are patched onto model instances in `patch_saving_functions()`:

| Method | Purpose | Format | Parameters |
|--------|---------|--------|-----------|
| `save_pretrained()` / `push_to_hub()` | Standard HF save | Native format (LoRA/merged) | Same as HF |
| `save_pretrained_merged()` | Save merged model (16/4-bit) | Native HF format | `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit" |
| `push_to_hub_merged()` | Push merged to HF Hub | Native HF format | Same + `repo_id` |
| `save_pretrained_gguf()` | Export to GGUF format | GGUF (via llama.cpp) | `quantization_method`, `first_conversion` |
| `push_to_hub_gguf()` | Export to GGUF & push | GGUF (via llama.cpp) | Same + `repo_id` |
| `save_pretrained_ggml()` | Convert LoRA to GGML | GGML format | Uses llama.cpp convert-lora-to-ggml |
| `push_to_hub_ggml()` | Convert LoRA to GGML & push | GGML format | Same + `repo_id` |
| `save_pretrained_torchao()` | Quantize with TorchAO | TorchAO format | `torchao_config` |

### 3. **Core Files & Key Function Locations**

#### **Main Save File**: `/unsloth/save.py`

**Key Functions:**

| Function | Lines | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `unsloth_save_model()` | 205-760 | **Core function** - Merges LoRA+4bit to weights, saves in HF format |
| `save_to_gguf()` | 952-1430+ | GGUF conversion orchestrator - manages llama.cpp pipeline |
| `save_to_gguf_generic()` | 2276-2352 | Generic GGUF export (vision models, non-LLM) |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | 2569-2678 | **Patches all methods onto model** using `types.MethodType` |
| `unsloth_generic_save()` | 2356-2410 | Wrapper for merged model saving - calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | 1768-1945 | Instance method - save GGUF locally |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | 1951-2140 | Instance method - save GGUF & push to Hub |
| `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` | 2413-2453 | Instance method - save merged model locally |
| `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` | 2456-2499 | Instance method - push merged model to Hub |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | 2211-2265 | Instance method - save LoRA as GGML |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | 2143-2209 | Instance method - push LoRA as GGML to Hub |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub()` | Dynamically generated | Wraps original `push_to_hub()` with Unsloth metadata |
| `_merge_lora()` | 159-187 | **Weight merging logic** - dequantizes 4-bit, merges LoRA (A.T @ B.T) |
| `upload_to_huggingface()` | 1495-1583 | Hub repo creation, model card generation, file upload |
| `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` | 1586-1615 | Handles BOS token in GGUF conversion |
| `create_ollama_modelfile()` | 1618-1661 | Generates Ollama-compatible modelfile |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | 2129-2140 | Saves LoRA adapters to custom directory |
| `get_executable()` | 937-949 | Locates system executables (llama-quantize, convert scripts) |

### 4. **How Save Methods Are Registered**

**Patching Flow:**

```python
# In patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False) [line 2569]
# 1. Create wrapper for push_to_hub()
exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())  # Dynamically generates unsloth_push_to_hub()
model.original_push_to_hub = original_push_to_hub
model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)

# 2. Register new save methods
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**When/Where Patching Happens:**
- Called from `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` / `FastVisionModel.from_pretrained()` in models/loader.py
- Also applied in trainer initialization via TRL integration

### 5. **Key Method Signatures & Docstrings**

#### **unsloth_save_model()** - The Core Merging Function
```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # "lora" | "merged_16bit" | "merged_4bit"
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    # Hub params
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    # Internal params
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]  # (save_directory, username)
```

#### **_merge_lora()** - Weight Merging Logic
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
    """
    Extracts and merges LoRA weights from a layer.
    Returns: (merged_weight, bias)
    
    For quantized layers:
    - Gets W (quantized weights), A (LoRA input), B (LoRA output), s (alpha)
    - Dequantizes W from 4-bit to float32
    - Performs: W = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))  using in-place addmm_
    - Converts back to original dtype
    
    For non-quantized layers:
    - Returns weight.data directly
    """
```

#### **unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()** - GGUF Instance Method
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    ... # standard HF save args
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
) -> None:
    """
    Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
    converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
    
    Supported quantization_method options:
    - "not_quantized": f16/f32, fast conversion, slow inference
    - "fast_quantized": q8_0 quantization
    - "quantized": q4_k_m quantization, slow conversion, fast inference
    - Individual quantization types: "f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", etc.
    """
```

#### **save_to_gguf()** - GGUF Conversion Pipeline
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,  # "float16" | "bfloat16"
    is_sentencepiece: bool = False,
    model_directory: str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method="fast_quantized",  # str | List[str]
    first_conversion: str = None,
    _run_installer=None,  # Non-blocking subprocess handle
) -> Tuple[List[str], bool]:  # (file_locations, want_full_precision)
    """
    Orchestrates GGUF export via llama.cpp pipeline:
    1. Installs llama.cpp (git clone, build with make/cmake)
    2. Converts HF model to GGUF 16-bit format
    3. Quantizes to requested format(s) using llama-quantize binary
    4. Returns paths to all generated GGUF files
    """
```

#### **patch_saving_functions()** - Method Registration
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False) -> model:
    """
    Patches model instance with Unsloth save/export methods.
    
    For non-vision models, patches:
    - save_pretrained_merged / push_to_hub_merged
    - save_pretrained_gguf / push_to_hub_gguf
    - save_pretrained_ggml / push_to_hub_ggml
    - save_pretrained_torchao
    
    For vision models, patches:
    - save_pretrained_merged / push_to_hub_merged
    - save_pretrained_gguf / push_to_hub_gguf (generic version)
    - save_pretrained_torchao
    
    Also wraps original push_to_hub() with Unsloth metadata injection.
    """
```

### 6. **Weight Extraction & Merging Patterns**

#### **LoRA Merging Pattern** (from `_merge_lora()`):

```python
# 1. Extract weights and LoRA parameters from layer
W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)

# 2. Dequantize if 4-bit quantized
if quant_state is not None:
    dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
    W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)  # From kernels module
else:
    dtype = W.dtype

# 3. Transpose and convert to float32
W = W.to(torch.float32).t()

# 4. Merge LoRA: W += (A.T @ (s * B.T))
if A is not None:
    W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    # Validate no NaN/inf
    if not torch.isfinite(torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())):
        raise ValueError(f"Merge failed. {name} has infinity elements.")

# 5. Transpose back and convert to original dtype
W = W.t().to(dtype)
```

#### **Merged Model State Dict Creation** (from `unsloth_save_model()`):

```python
# 1. Initialize ordered dict
state_dict = OrderedDict()

# 2. Get target dtype from config
torch_dtype = dtype_from_config(internal_model.config)  # float16 or bfloat16

# 3. Add embedding layer
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = internal_model.model.embed_tokens.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)

# 4. Iterate through all layers and merge LoRA
for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    # Merge attention weights (Q, K, V, O projections)
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", ...]
        W, bias = _merge_lora(layer.self_attn.{item}, name)
        state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.self_attn.{item}.weight"] = W
        if bias is not None:
            state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.self_attn.{item}.bias"] = bias
    
    # Add layer normalization weights (no LoRA)
    for item in LLAMA_LAYERNORMS:  # ["input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm"]
        state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"] = layer.{item}.weight.data

# 5. Add final norm and LM head
state_dict["model.norm.weight"] = internal_model.model.norm.weight.data
if not tied_weights:
    state_dict["lm_head.weight"] = internal_model.lm_head.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)

# 6. Save to disk with HF's save_pretrained()
internal_model.save_pretrained(**save_pretrained_settings)
```

### 7. **GGUF Export Pipeline**

**Files Involved:**
- `save_to_gguf()` - Main orchestrator
- Calls external `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp.convert_to_gguf()` and `install_llama_cpp()`
- Uses llama.cpp binary tools: `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`, `llama-quantize`

**Quantization Methods Supported:**
```python
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized": dtype (f32/f16),
    "fast_quantized": "q8_0",
    "quantized": "q4_k_m",
    # Individual methods:
    "f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0",
    "q4_k_m", "q4_k_s", "q4_k", "q4_0", "q4_1",
    "q5_k_m", "q5_k_s", "q5_k", "q5_0", "q5_1",
    "q6_k", "q2_k", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_xs",
}
```

### 8. **Adapter Saving**

**LoRA Adapter Saving (native HF format):**
```python
# save_method="lora" in unsloth_save_model()
# Calls model.save_pretrained(...) with PEFT's LoRA format
# Generates: adapter_config.json, adapter_model.bin
# This is the default and fastest method
```

**LoRA to GGML Conversion:**
```python
save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)
  ↓
unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="lora")
  ↓
External: python llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {dir} {output.bin} llama
  ↓
Result: ggml-adapter-model.bin
```

### 9. **Hub Integration**

**Key Hub Functions:**
- `_determine_username()` - Parses repo ID and gets HF username from token
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Creates repo, generates model card, uploads files
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` - Generates Ollama-compatible modelfile for GGUF
- `MODEL_CARD` template - Standard model card with Unsloth branding

**Flow for push_to_hub:**
```
push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, ...)
  ↓
unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit", push_to_hub=False)
  ↓
save_to_gguf(...)  # Creates GGUF files locally
  ↓
upload_to_huggingface(model, repo_id, token, ...)
  ↓
HfApi.upload_file() for each GGUF file
```

### 10. **Memory Management Strategy**

```python
# Adaptive saving based on available RAM
max_ram = psutil.virtual_memory().available
if safe_serialization:
    max_ram -= 5GB  # safetensors needs more RAM
else:
    max_ram -= 1.25GB  # PyTorch needs less

max_ram *= maximum_memory_usage  # Default 0.9

# For each weight:
if (torch.cuda.memory_allocated() + W.nbytes) < max_vram:
    state_dict[name] = W  # Save to VRAM
elif (max_ram - W.nbytes) > 0:
    state_dict[name] = W.to("cpu")  # Save to RAM
else:
    torch.save(W, temp_file)  # Spill to disk
    state_dict[name] = torch.load(temp_file, mmap=True)  # Load with mmap
```

### 11. **Related Imported Functions from unsloth_zoo**

These are imported but defined elsewhere:
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` - From `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`
- `convert_to_gguf()` - From `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`
- `install_llama_cpp()` - From `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`
- `dtype_from_config()` - From `unsloth_zoo.hf_utils`
- `get_lora_parameters_bias()` - From `unsloth.kernels`
- `fast_dequantize()` - From `unsloth.kernels`

---

### **Summary Table of All Exports**

| What Gets Exported | File Location | Public Signature | When Used |
|-------------------|----------------|-----------------|-----------|
| `print_quantization_methods` | save.py:109 | `()` | User calls directly |
| `unsloth_save_model` | save.py:205 | Full params | Internal/advanced users |
| `save_to_gguf` | save.py:952 | Full params | Internal (called by instance methods) |
| `patch_saving_functions` | save.py:2569 | `(model, vision=False)` | Internal (called by loader) |
| `create_huggingface_repo` | save.py:1456 | `(model, save_dir, token, private)` | Advanced users |

**Instance Methods Added to Model:**
- `.save_pretrained()` - Wrapped original with Unsloth tags

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report with all my findings.

## Unsloth Codebase - Complete Save/Export Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the Unsloth codebase and mapped out the entire save/export system. Here's my comprehensive report:

### 1. **Directory Structure**

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                    # Main entry point - imports save, models, trainer
├── save.py                        # CORE FILE: 2678 lines - all save/export logic
├── trainer.py                     # Training integration, TRL patching
├── chat_templates.py
├── tokenizer_utils.py
├── models/
│   ├── __init__.py               # Imports FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, etc.
│   ├── llama.py
│   ├── mistral.py
│   ├── loader.py                 # FastLanguageModel and FastVisionModel
│   ├── rl.py
│   ├── _utils.py
│   └── [other model architectures]
├── kernels/                       # Contains fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE
├── registry/
├── utils/
└── dataprep/
```

### 2. **Save Methods Overview**

The system exposes **6 primary save methods** that are patched onto model instances in `patch_saving_functions()`:

| Method | Purpose | Format | Parameters |
|--------|---------|--------|-----------|
| `save_pretrained()` / `push_to_hub()` | Standard HF save | Native format (LoRA/merged) | Same as HF |
| `save_pretrained_merged()` | Save merged model (16/4-bit) | Native HF format | `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit" |
| `push_to_hub_merged()` | Push merged to HF Hub | Native HF format | Same + `repo_id` |
| `save_pretrained_gguf()` | Export to GGUF format | GGUF (via llama.cpp) | `quantization_method`, `first_conversion` |
| `push_to_hub_gguf()` | Export to GGUF & push | GGUF (via llama.cpp) | Same + `repo_id` |
| `save_pretrained_ggml()` | Convert LoRA to GGML | GGML format | Uses llama.cpp convert-lora-to-ggml |
| `push_to_hub_ggml()` | Convert LoRA to GGML & push | GGML format | Same + `repo_id` |
| `save_pretrained_torchao()` | Quantize with TorchAO | TorchAO format | `torchao_config` |

### 3. **Core Files & Key Function Locations**

#### **Main Save File**: `/unsloth/save.py`

**Key Functions:**

| Function | Lines | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `unsloth_save_model()` | 205-760 | **Core function** - Merges LoRA+4bit to weights, saves in HF format |
| `save_to_gguf()` | 952-1430+ | GGUF conversion orchestrator - manages llama.cpp pipeline |
| `save_to_gguf_generic()` | 2276-2352 | Generic GGUF export (vision models, non-LLM) |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | 2569-2678 | **Patches all methods onto model** using `types.MethodType` |
| `unsloth_generic_save()` | 2356-2410 | Wrapper for merged model saving - calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | 1768-1945 | Instance method - save GGUF locally |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | 1951-2140 | Instance method - save GGUF & push to Hub |
| `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` | 2413-2453 | Instance method - save merged model locally |
| `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` | 2456-2499 | Instance method - push merged model to Hub |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | 2211-2265 | Instance method - save LoRA as GGML |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | 2143-2209 | Instance method - push LoRA as GGML to Hub |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub()` | Dynamically generated | Wraps original `push_to_hub()` with Unsloth metadata |
| `_merge_lora()` | 159-187 | **Weight merging logic** - dequantizes 4-bit, merges LoRA (A.T @ B.T) |
| `upload_to_huggingface()` | 1495-1583 | Hub repo creation, model card generation, file upload |
| `fix_tokenizer_bos_token()` | 1586-1615 | Handles BOS token in GGUF conversion |
| `create_ollama_modelfile()` | 1618-1661 | Generates Ollama-compatible modelfile |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | 2129-2140 | Saves LoRA adapters to custom directory |
| `get_executable()` | 937-949 | Locates system executables (llama-quantize, convert scripts) |

### 4. **How Save Methods Are Registered**

**Patching Flow:**

```python
# In patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False) [line 2569]
# 1. Create wrapper for push_to_hub()
exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())  # Dynamically generates unsloth_push_to_hub()
model.original_push_to_hub = original_push_to_hub
model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, model)

# 2. Register new save methods
model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**When/Where Patching Happens:**
- Called from `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` / `FastVisionModel.from_pretrained()` in models/loader.py
- Also applied in trainer initialization via TRL integration

### 5. **Key Method Signatures & Docstrings**

#### **unsloth_save_model()** - The Core Merging Function
```python
@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # "lora" | "merged_16bit" | "merged_4bit"
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    # Hub params
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    # Internal params
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]  # (save_directory, username)
```

#### **_merge_lora()** - Weight Merging Logic
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
    """
    Extracts and merges LoRA weights from a layer.
    Returns: (merged_weight, bias)
    
    For quantized layers:
    - Gets W (quantized weights), A (LoRA input), B (LoRA output), s (alpha)
    - Dequantizes W from 4-bit to float32
    - Performs: W = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))  using in-place addmm_
    - Converts back to original dtype
    
    For non-quantized layers:
    - Returns weight.data directly
    """
```

#### **unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()** - GGUF Instance Method
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    ... # standard HF save args
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
) -> None:
    """
    Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
    converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
    
    Supported quantization_method options:
    - "not_quantized": f16/f32, fast conversion, slow inference
    - "fast_quantized": q8_0 quantization
    - "quantized": q4_k_m quantization, slow conversion, fast inference
    - Individual quantization types: "f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", etc.
    """
```

#### **save_to_gguf()** - GGUF Conversion Pipeline
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type: str,
    model_dtype: str,  # "float16" | "bfloat16"
    is_sentencepiece: bool = False,
    model_directory: str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method="fast_quantized",  # str | List[str]
    first_conversion: str = None,
    _run_installer=None,  # Non-blocking subprocess handle
) -> Tuple[List[str], bool]:  # (file_locations, want_full_precision)
    """
    Orchestrates GGUF export via llama.cpp pipeline:
    1. Installs llama.cpp (git clone, build with make/cmake)
    2. Converts HF model to GGUF 16-bit format
    3. Quantizes to requested format(s) using llama-quantize binary
    4. Returns paths to all generated GGUF files
    """
```

#### **patch_saving_functions()** - Method Registration
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False) -> model:
    """
    Patches model instance with Unsloth save/export methods.
    
    For non-vision models, patches:
    - save_pretrained_merged / push_to_hub_merged
    - save_pretrained_gguf / push_to_hub_gguf
    - save_pretrained_ggml / push_to_hub_ggml
    - save_pretrained_torchao
    
    For vision models, patches:
    - save_pretrained_merged / push_to_hub_merged
    - save_pretrained_gguf / push_to_hub_gguf (generic version)
    - save_pretrained_torchao
    
    Also wraps original push_to_hub() with Unsloth metadata injection.
    """
```

### 6. **Weight Extraction & Merging Patterns**

#### **LoRA Merging Pattern** (from `_merge_lora()`):

```python
# 1. Extract weights and LoRA parameters from layer
W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)

# 2. Dequantize if 4-bit quantized
if quant_state is not None:
    dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
    W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)  # From kernels module
else:
    dtype = W.dtype

# 3. Transpose and convert to float32
W = W.to(torch.float32).t()

# 4. Merge LoRA: W += (A.T @ (s * B.T))
if A is not None:
    W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    # Validate no NaN/inf
    if not torch.isfinite(torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())):
        raise ValueError(f"Merge failed. {name} has infinity elements.")

# 5. Transpose back and convert to original dtype
W = W.t().to(dtype)
```

#### **Merged Model State Dict Creation** (from `unsloth_save_model()`):

```python
# 1. Initialize ordered dict
state_dict = OrderedDict()

# 2. Get target dtype from config
torch_dtype = dtype_from_config(internal_model.config)  # float16 or bfloat16

# 3. Add embedding layer
state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"] = internal_model.model.embed_tokens.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)

# 4. Iterate through all layers and merge LoRA
for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
    # Merge attention weights (Q, K, V, O projections)
    for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:  # ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", ...]
        W, bias = _merge_lora(layer.self_attn.{item}, name)
        state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.self_attn.{item}.weight"] = W
        if bias is not None:
            state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.self_attn.{item}.bias"] = bias
    
    # Add layer normalization weights (no LoRA)
    for item in LLAMA_LAYERNORMS:  # ["input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm"]
        state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item}.weight"] = layer.{item}.weight.data

# 5. Add final norm and LM head
state_dict["model.norm.weight"] = internal_model.model.norm.weight.data
if not tied_weights:
    state_dict["lm_head.weight"] = internal_model.lm_head.weight.data.to(torch_dtype)

# 6. Save to disk with HF's save_pretrained()
internal_model.save_pretrained(**save_pretrained_settings)
```

### 7. **GGUF Export Pipeline**

**Files Involved:**
- `save_to_gguf()` - Main orchestrator
- Calls external `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp.convert_to_gguf()` and `install_llama_cpp()`
- Uses llama.cpp binary tools: `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`, `llama-quantize`

**Quantization Methods Supported:**
```python
ALLOWED_QUANTS = {
    "not_quantized": dtype (f32/f16),
    "fast_quantized": "q8_0",
    "quantized": "q4_k_m",
    # Individual methods:
    "f32", "f16", "bf16", "q8_0",
    "q4_k_m", "q4_k_s", "q4_k", "q4_0", "q4_1",
    "q5_k_m", "q5_k_s", "q5_k", "q5_0", "q5_1",
    "q6_k", "q2_k", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_xs",
}
```

### 8. **Adapter Saving**

**LoRA Adapter Saving (native HF format):**
```python
# save_method="lora" in unsloth_save_model()
# Calls model.save_pretrained(...) with PEFT's LoRA format
# Generates: adapter_config.json, adapter_model.bin
# This is the default and fastest method
```

**LoRA to GGML Conversion:**
```python
save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)
  ↓
unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="lora")
  ↓
External: python llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {dir} {output.bin} llama
  ↓
Result: ggml-adapter-model.bin
```

### 9. **Hub Integration**

**Key Hub Functions:**
- `_determine_username()` - Parses repo ID and gets HF username from token
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Creates repo, generates model card, uploads files
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` - Generates Ollama-compatible modelfile for GGUF
- `MODEL_CARD` template - Standard model card with Unsloth branding

**Flow for push_to_hub:**
```
push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, ...)
  ↓
unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit", push_to_hub=False)
  ↓
save_to_gguf(...)  # Creates GGUF files locally
  ↓
upload_to_huggingface(model, repo_id, token, ...)
  ↓
HfApi.upload_file() for each GGUF file
```

### 10. **Memory Management Strategy**

```python
# Adaptive saving based on available RAM
max_ram = psutil.virtual_memory().available
if safe_serialization:
    max_ram -= 5GB  # safetensors needs more RAM
else:
    max_ram -= 1.25GB  # PyTorch needs less

max_ram *= maximum_memory_usage  # Default 0.9

# For each weight:
if (torch.cuda.memory_allocated() + W.nbytes) < max_vram:
    state_dict[name] = W  # Save to VRAM
elif (max_ram - W.nbytes) > 0:
    state_dict[name] = W.to("cpu")  # Save to RAM
else:
    torch.save(W, temp_file)  # Spill to disk
    state_dict[name] = torch.load(temp_file, mmap=True)  # Load with mmap
```

### 11. **Related Imported Functions from unsloth_zoo**

These are imported but defined elsewhere:
- `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` - From `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`
- `convert_to_gguf()` - From `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`
- `install_llama_cpp()` - From `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`
- `dtype_from_config()` - From `unsloth_zoo.hf_utils`
- `get_lora_parameters_bias()` - From `unsloth.kernels`
- `fast_dequantize()` - From `unsloth.kernels`

---

### **Summary Table of All Exports**

| What Gets Exported | File Location | Public Signature | When Used |
|-------------------|----------------|-----------------|-----------|
| `print_quantization_methods` | save.py:109 | `()` | User calls directly |
| `unsloth_save_model` | save.py:205 | Full params | Internal/advanced users |
| `save_to_gguf` | save.py:952 | Full params | Internal (called by instance methods) |
| `patch_saving_functions` | save.py:2569 | `(model, vision=False)` | Internal (called by loader) |
| `create_huggingface_repo` | save.py:1456 | `(model, save_dir, token, private)` | Advanced users |

**Instance Methods Added to Model:**
- `.save_pretrained()` - Wrapped original with Unsloth tags
- `.push_to_hub()` - Wrapped original with Unsloth tags  
- `.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`
- `.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`
- `.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method, ...)`
- `.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method, ...)`
- `.save_pretrained_ggml(save_directory, tokenizer, ...)`
- `.push_to_hub_ggml(repo_id, tokenizer, ...)`
- `.save_pretrained_torchao(save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config, ...)`

from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames
KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"
del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
    # "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass
pass


def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False

except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

# Fix other issues
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue

ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation

            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass


@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()

def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass


# INTEL GPU Specific Logic
if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
    @torch.inference_mode
    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
        if quant_state is None: return W
        if type(quant_state) is not list:
            # New quant_state as a class
            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
            shape      = quant_state.shape

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. I need to add an `unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx` function to `save.py` that:
   - Extracts LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) from named modules
   - Creates ONNX-compatible representation (using `torch.onnx` or manually constructing ONNX nodes)
   - Saves to a specified output directory
   - Includes metadata (rank, alpha, target modules)

2. Register/expose it via `patch_saving_functions()`


3. Update `__all__` in save.py

For the ONNX export approach, I'm thinking about how to represent the LoRA adapter weights themselves rather than the full merged model. For each adapter module, I need to extract the A and B matrices along with the scaling factor, then construct an ONNX graph that encodes the LoRA computation where the output applies the adapter transformation to the input.

The most practical approach would be creating one ONNX file per adapter module, capturing the linear transformations through A and B matrices with the appropriate scaling applied. I'm weighing different approaches for exporting LoRA adapters to ONNX format. The most practical solution is to create a manifest file alongside individual ONNX files for each adapter module, where each file encodes the LoRA computation as a small graph that applies the delta transformation using the A and B matrices scaled by the adapter's alpha value.

Now I'm examining the PEFT model structure to understand how the LoRA weights are organized—each adapter layer contains lora_A and lora_B weight matrices with specific shapes, plus a scaling factor derived from alpha and rank. For each LoRA layer in the model, I'll create a corresponding ONNX graph that takes the input, applies the low-rank update computation...

Building the ONNX graph structure... Writing ONNX graph nodes... Building the ONNX model...

I'm reconsidering the export structure—it makes more sense to save each LoRA adapter as its own ONNX file rather than bundling them together, with a JSON metadata file alongside for tracking. I'll name each file based on the sanitized layer name and place them in the output directory, then implement the actual export logic.

Writing the ONNX export function... *, out_features] ✓

So the ONNX graph needs to chain two Gemm operations: first apply lora_A with transB=1 to get the intermediate result, then apply lora_B with transB=1 to that result, and finally scale by alpha before adding to the original output. I'm realizing that `Gemm` won't work here since the input might be 3D with batch and sequence dimensions, so I need to switch to `MatMul` instead, which handles arbitrary shapes. def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
